summary: Iteration 2 — tested new Stripe Checkout integration + audio/notification cues, plus regression of iteration_1 flows. Backend: 29/29 pytest pass (22 regression + 7 new Stripe). All new payment endpoints work as designed including server-side amount recomputation (anti-tamper verified: tampered prices were ignored, Stripe session amount matched menu prices). Frontend: 6/7 critical new flows pass — Pay button correctly shows 'Pay ₹735 with Stripe', /api/payment/checkout/session network call fires and returns valid cs_test_… URL on https://checkout.stripe.com, kitchen-toggle-sound + kitchen-enable-notif render and toggle works ('SOUND ON' ↔ 'SOUND OFF'), payment-return polling UI renders. ❌ CRITICAL React hooks violation on /customer/track/[orderId] crashes the page after the order data loads — useRef/useEffect/useState/useEffect hooks are placed AFTER an early `if (!order) return …` at line 27, violating the Rules of Hooks. Result: track-enable-notif button + track-stages never render; runtime error 'Rendered more hooks than during the previous render' shown in Next dev overlay.

ui_bugs.issue: Rules-of-Hooks violation: hooks (useRef line 33, useEffect line 34, useState line 43, useEffect line 44) are called AFTER an early conditional return `if (!order) return …` on line 27. On first render order is undefined → only 1 hook (useQuery) runs; on second render after data arrives → 5+ hooks run → React throws 'Rendered more hooks than during the previous render' and unmounts the page. track-enable-notif button and track-stages never render for end users.

action_items: FIX TrackPage hooks order: move all hooks (useRef, both useEffects, useState) ABOVE the `if (!order) return …` guard at line 27. Pattern: declare every hook unconditionally at the top of the component, then do the early return, then derive `currentIdx`/`idx` and render. Inside useEffect, keep the `if (!order) return` guard — that's safe because useEffect itself was unconditionally called.

(Optional polish) Increase test wait if reproducing payment-return fail state for invalid session — the page correctly enters the 'Confirming…' polling state; the timeout-to-fail transition was not observed within 8s in this run.
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL: TrackPage violates Rules of Hooks — see action_items. This is a regression introduced by the notification work in this iteration.

/api/payment/checkout/session endpoint correctly recomputes amount server-side from menu prices (verified by tampering: client-supplied price 0.01 was ignored; Stripe session amount_total matched (subtotal*1.05)*100 paise within rounding).
/api/payment/checkout/status/{session_id} correctly returns 404 for unknown session and 'unpaid' for fresh sessions; idempotently materializes order only on first transition to 'paid'.
Legacy /api/payment/intent mock endpoint preserved — backward compat intact.
Console hydration warnings 'Expected server HTML to contain a matching <span> in <a>' from CustomerLayout — likely a Next.js Link wrapping warning; cosmetic dev-only warning, not a blocker.
server.py is now 762 lines (was 573) — exceeds the 700-line soft threshold; main agent should consider splitting payment endpoints into a dedicated router (e.g., backend/routers/payment.py).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_smartdine.py (added 7 Stripe-related tests: test_payment_config, test_create_checkout_session, test_checkout_session_invalid_item, test_checkout_session_empty, test_checkout_status_unpaid, test_checkout_status_unknown_session, test_server_recomputes_amount)
test_credentials: owner@smartdine.ai/Owner@123, chef@smartdine.ai/Chef@123, counter@smartdine.ai/Counter@123, guest@smartdine.ai/Guest@123 (unchanged from iteration_1)
seed_data_creation: Created additional pending payment_transactions records (TEST_Stripe, TEST_CurlVerify) — these stay in 'pending' state and do not pollute the orders collection (materialization only on paid status).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes TrackPage hooks order, re-test by: (1) create an order via POST /api/orders, (2) navigate to /customer/track/{order_id}, (3) verify no console error, (4) verify [data-testid='track-enable-notif'] and [data-testid='track-stages'] are visible. Also exercise status transition (chef login → patch /api/orders/{id}/status to 'ready') to trigger the playChime('ready') + notify() code path. Backend payment flow is fully verified — focus next iteration on the TrackPage fix + any Supabase migration if main agent attempts it.
rca of the issue: TrackPage hooks bug — RCA: When adding the new notification/sound features in this iteration, useRef/useEffect/useState/useEffect calls were appended at lines 33-46, but the pre-existing early-return guard `if (!order) return <Loading/>` at line 27 was NOT moved above them. React requires hooks to be called in the same order on every render. On the first render (order undefined) only useQuery runs and the component returns early; on the second render (order present) all 5+ hooks run → mismatch → React unmounts the tree with 'Rendered more hooks than during the previous render'. Mitigation: move ALL hooks to the very top of the function body (before any conditional return); the existing guards inside useEffect bodies (`if (!order) return`) already handle the undefined case safely.
